articles.title: Andys Man Club Attends Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show
articles.excerpt: Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale were guests at the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, where they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 20 July 2026 at 00:07. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale were guests at the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, where they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana.

Latest update

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show as guests of the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event provided an opportunity for the group to engage with the community and promote their initiatives.

During the show, they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana and his wife, who expressed their support for the work of Andys Man Club. The club focuses on mental health awareness and support for men in the community.

The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts and families alike. It served as a platform for local organisations to connect with residents and share information about their activities.

Andys Man Club Rochdale continues to encourage open discussions about mental health and aims to reduce stigma surrounding these issues. The support from local leaders like Mayor Rana is seen as vital for their ongoing efforts.

Earlier developments

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, hosted by the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts from the local area.

During the show, members met the newly appointed Mayor of Rochdale, Faisal Rana, and his wife. The Mayor expressed support for the initiatives of Andys Man Club, which focuses on men's mental health and wellbeing.

The presence of Andys Man Club at the event highlights the group's commitment to raising awareness about mental health issues among men in the community. Their participation in local events fosters connections and encourages open discussions.

The Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show provided an opportunity for local organisations to engage with the community and promote their causes. Events like this are important for building community spirit and support.

Update timeline

  • 20 July 2026, 00:07: Andys Man Club Attends Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show
  • 19 July 2026, 20:16: Andys Man Club Attends Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show

articles.title: Missing Maltese Dog in Trub, Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A Maltese dog has been reported missing since 10th July from Thornham New Road in Trub, Rochdale. The dog is female, white, and has a small scar on her belly.
articles.content_html:
A Maltese dog has been reported missing since 10th July on Thornham New Road in Trub, Rochdale. The dog is female and white, with a small scar on her belly.

The dog's details are listed under Dog ID 204129 on the Dog Lost website, where further information can be found.

Anyone who may have seen the dog or has information regarding her whereabouts is encouraged to check the Dog Lost website for updates.

The missing dog may be affecting local residents who are concerned about her safety and wellbeing.

articles.title: Patients in Deprived Areas Wait Longer for NHS Treatment
articles.excerpt: New figures reveal that patients in deprived areas of Rochdale are experiencing longer wait times for NHS treatment compared to those in more affluent regions.
articles.content_html:
New figures have revealed that patients in deprived areas of Rochdale are waiting longer for NHS treatment than those in more affluent regions. The data indicates a growing disparity in access to timely healthcare services.

Individuals living in less affluent neighbourhoods face increased waiting times for essential medical procedures and consultations. This trend raises concerns about health inequalities within the community.

Health officials have noted that the situation may be exacerbated by factors such as staffing shortages and increased demand for services in these areas.

Local health authorities are expected to review the data and consider strategies to improve access to care for residents in deprived areas.

articles.title: New Gentle Exercise Programme for Older Adults Launched
articles.excerpt: A new gentle exercise programme aimed at older adults has been launched to promote health and wellbeing in Rochdale.
articles.content_html:
A new gentle exercise programme for older adults has been launched in Rochdale. The initiative aims to encourage physical activity among seniors, promoting health and wellbeing in the community.

The programme is designed to be accessible and enjoyable, catering to the needs of older participants. Sessions will focus on low-impact exercises that can be performed safely and comfortably.

Local health officials have emphasised the importance of staying active as people age, highlighting the benefits of regular exercise for both physical and mental health.

The programme is expected to run weekly, providing a supportive environment for older adults to engage in physical activity and social interaction.

articles.title: Impact Gym Opens New Facility in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Impact Gym has opened a new facility in Rochdale, enhancing fitness options for local residents with state-of-the-art equipment and diverse classes.
articles.content_html:
Impact Gym has opened a new facility in Rochdale, providing additional fitness options for local residents. The gym is located on the outskirts of the town centre and aims to cater to a diverse range of fitness enthusiasts.

The new facility features state-of-the-art equipment and group classes. This expansion is part of the gym's commitment to promoting health and wellness in the community.

Members of the gym have expressed excitement about the new offerings, which include a variety of classes designed for different fitness levels.

The opening of the facility may increase foot traffic in the area, encouraging residents to visit and explore the new services available.

articles.title: Local Fitness Classes to Feature High Intensity Interval Training
articles.excerpt: A series of fitness classes featuring High Intensity Interval Training will be held in Rochdale, offering residents a chance to improve their fitness levels.
articles.content_html:
A series of fitness classes featuring High Intensity Interval Training will take place in Rochdale. The sessions aim to provide an effective workout for participants looking to enhance their fitness levels.

The classes, organised by Your Trust, will focus on high-intensity exercises designed to improve cardiovascular health and overall strength. Participants of all fitness levels are welcome to join.

In addition to High Intensity Interval Training, other fitness options such as core training will also be available, allowing residents to choose classes that suit their individual needs.

These initiatives are part of a broader effort to promote health and wellness within the community.

articles.title: Rochdale's Adult Care STARS Service Receives 'Good' Rating from CQC
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council’s adult care STARS service has been rated ‘good’ by the Care Quality Commission, recognising its support for vulnerable residents.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 15 July 2026 at 16:57. 4 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale Borough Council’s adult care STARS service has been rated ‘good’ by the Care Quality Commission, recognising its support for vulnerable residents.

Latest update

Rochdale Borough Council’s adult care STARS service has been rated ‘good’ by the Care Quality Commission, recognising its support for vulnerable residents.

Rochdale Borough Council's STARS adult care service has been rated 'good' by the Care Quality Commission (CQC) following its latest inspection. The service supports over 1,200 vulnerable residents each year after they leave hospital, assisting them in maintaining their independence at home.

The CQC assessment found that the STARS service is well-led and delivers effective, caring, and responsive support for people living in Heywood, Middleton, the Pennines, and Rochdale. Inspectors praised the staff for treating residents with kindness, empathy, and compassion, helping them lead healthier lives while maintaining their independence, choice, and control.

The report highlighted the service's commitment to placing individuals at the centre of their care decisions, effective partnership working, and strong communication with families and other care providers. This assessment follows a similar 'good' rating for the council's wider adult care service, which also reflects the quality of care provided across the borough.

Earlier developments

Councillor Shah Wazir, cabinet member for adult care, expressed pride in the STARS service, stating that the CQC report reflects the high-quality care provided to thousands of residents. He thanked the STARS team for their outstanding work and noted the service's role in reducing health inequalities and freeing up valuable hospital beds. The STARS service has been supporting residents across the borough since 2008.

Rochdale Borough Council’s adult care STARS service has received a ‘good’ rating following a recent assessment by the Care Quality Commission (CQC). This rating reflects the quality of care provided to vulnerable residents in the borough.

The Short Term Assessment and Reablement Service (STARS) supports over 1,200 residents each year, assisting them in maintaining independence after hospital stays. The CQC commended STARS for being well-led and delivering high standards of care in Heywood, Middleton, Pennines, and Rochdale.

Inspectors highlighted the effectiveness, caring nature, and responsiveness of the service, noting that staff treated residents with kindness, empathy, and compassion. The report praised the team for helping residents lead healthier lives while maximising their independence, choice, and control.

Councillor Shah Wazir, the council’s cabinet member for adult care, stated that the CQC report is a testament to the valuable service STARS provides to thousands of people in the borough, emphasising the appreciation expressed by constituents for the compassionate care offered.

Rochdale Borough Council has received positive feedback from the Care Quality Commission (CQC) regarding its adult care services. The CQC's latest report highlighted significant improvements in the quality of care provided to residents.

The council's adult care services were assessed on various criteria, including safety, effectiveness, and responsiveness. The CQC noted that the services have made considerable progress in meeting the needs of the community.

Councillor Shah Wazir expressed pride in the recognition, stating that the council is committed to enhancing the quality of care for all residents.

Update timeline

  • 15 July 2026, 16:57: Rochdale's Adult Care STARS Service Receives 'Good' Rating from CQC

articles.title: Heads Together Initiative Launched in Rochdale Schools
articles.excerpt: A new initiative called Heads Together has been launched in Rochdale to support mental health in schools, providing resources and training for staff and students.
articles.content_html:
A new initiative called Heads Together has been launched in Rochdale to enhance mental health support in schools. The programme aims to provide resources and training for staff and students, focusing on creating a supportive environment for mental well-being.

Heads Together will involve workshops and training sessions designed to help schools address mental health issues effectively. The initiative seeks to equip educators with the tools necessary to identify and support students facing mental health challenges.

Local education leaders have expressed their support for the initiative, highlighting the importance of mental health awareness in schools.

Further details on the initiative will be shared with schools in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Your Trust Launches Body Balance Programme in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Your Trust has introduced a new Body Balance programme aimed at improving health and wellbeing in Rochdale. The initiative encourages community participation and offers various classes.
articles.content_html:
Your Trust has launched a new programme called Body Balance, designed to enhance health and wellbeing in the Rochdale area. The initiative aims to promote physical activity through a series of classes.

The Body Balance programme includes sessions tailored to different fitness levels, encouraging residents to participate and improve their overall health. Classes will be held at various locations across Rochdale.

Participants can engage in activities that focus on flexibility, strength, and relaxation, making it suitable for individuals of all ages.

For more information on the Body Balance programme and how to join, residents can visit the Your Trust website or contact their local community centre.

articles.title: Pilates Classes Now Available in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A new series of Pilates classes has been launched in Rochdale by Your Trust, designed to enhance fitness and promote overall wellbeing among participants.
articles.content_html:
A new series of Pilates classes has been launched in Rochdale by Your Trust. The classes aim to enhance fitness and promote overall wellbeing among participants.

Sessions will cater to various skill levels, ensuring that both beginners and experienced participants can benefit from the exercises. The classes will focus on core strength, flexibility, and posture improvement.

Classes are scheduled at convenient times throughout the week, making it easier for residents to incorporate them into their routines. Interested individuals can find more details on timings and registration on the Your Trust website.

This initiative provides residents with an opportunity to engage in healthy practices.
